content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't collaborate
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome collaboration bottlenecks. A high-level guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'can't collaborate' bottleneck vanishes when you standardize your architecture. By deploying a modular SaaS starter kit, you unify your codebase, enforce strict type safety, and provide clients with a predictable environment. Using Antigravity for scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic, you can transform chaotic handoffs into seamless, professional deployments.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen, waiting for a client to understand why their custom-hacked codebase is unmaintainable. The friction is palpable; you can't collaborate effectively because your environments are fragmented, documentation is non-existent, and the deployment process is a minefield. You are currently trading your sanity for billable hours.

But the tide is turning. High-performing freelancers aren't coding from scratch; they are architecting ecosystems. By leveraging a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your time and offer your clients a robust, scalable foundation that turns a one-off project into a long-term partnership. It’s time to move from manual labor to automated mastery.

steps.title: Standardize the Architecture
steps.description: Establish a unified folder structure and authentication layer to ensure all future projects speak the same language.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular Next.js 14 boilerplate with Supabase auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS, structured for team scalability.

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe Contracts
steps.description: Define Prisma or Drizzle schemas early to prevent collaboration drift between database and frontend logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application with clear role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema with user, organization, and subscription models'

steps.title: Automate API Documentation
steps.description: Use automated documentation to ensure clients and future collaborators can understand your endpoints without asking questions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup Swagger/OpenAPI integration for all Next.js API routes within the starter kit.

steps.title: Configure CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Eliminate 'it works on my machine' issues by setting up automated deployment previews for every pull request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for Vercel deployment, including linting, testing, and preview environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create .github/workflows/deploy.yml with automated testing and preview urls'

steps.title: Standardize Component Library
steps.description: Use a shared component library to ensure the UI remains consistent, regardless of who is editing the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a Shadcn/UI integration with a custom theme configuration for rapid UI prototyping.

pro_tips: Always wrap your database calls in a custom service layer so you can swap providers without breaking the UI.
Use environment variable validation (like Zod) to crash builds immediately if a config is missing.
Implement 'feature flags' early to allow clients to toggle unfinished features safely in production.
Never hardcode strings; use a centralized localization file, even for single-language apps, for easier future scaling.
Treat your starter kit as a product: version it using Git tags so you can update clients to newer versions without friction.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is initialized, document your 'standard workflow' in a README.md file so your future self (or your team) has a single source of truth.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start building a high-margin freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
